headline: Record ETF inflows H1 2026 - money still flowing into tech
detail: Institutional and retail money piling into ETFs at record pace means continued bid under QQQ, SMH, and your semi names. However, record inflows can signal a crowded trade - when sentiment flips, the exit is narrow. Your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) benefit most on the way up but face the sharpest unwind.
rec: No action needed now - but this confirms your stops matter more than ever when everyone's on the same side of the boat. Watch SMH for any distribution signals.

headline: Oil posts largest quarterly drop in 6 years
detail: Falling energy costs are a macro tailwind for tech - lower input costs, less inflation pressure, gives the Fed room. Deflationary oil supports the multiple-expansion story that's been driving your chip names. Not a direct catalyst but a friendly backdrop.
rec: No specific trade here - it's a supportive macro condition, not a trigger. Hold your positions and let it work as a tailwind.

headline: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program
detail: More big tech bets on AI infrastructure signals continued demand for compute - directly positive for the chip ecosystem your book lives in (CRWD, SMH, SOXL all benefit from sustained AI capex narrative). Not a single-stock event but reinforces the sector thesis.
rec: No action today, but this supports holding SMH and SOXL through normal volatility. If NVDA dips to the $198-199 scanner entry zone, that's the name most directly tied to AI compute demand - scanner entry $198.39-198.62, stop $197.35, though it's a grade-C setup so size small if you go there.

headline: Michael Burry shorting Caterpillar after AI-driven rally
detail: Burry's bet signals he thinks the AI-driven industrial rally is overdone - not directly your names, but it's a notable contrarian flag on whether the broader AI-halo trade is stretched. Your book is priced for perfection; a sentiment shift in AI enthusiasm hits tech/semis hardest.
rec: No trade - you don't own CAT. But treat this as a yellow flag: if a high-profile short seller thinks the AI rally is overextended in industrials, your semi names sitting at premium levels deserve tight stops, not looser ones.
